mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market, indicating strong consumer demand and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate continued economic strength.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) when UNRATE is below its 10-year moving average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as market participants price in improved financial performance.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return in triggered periods is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is elevated and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, it signals aggressive monetary tightening to combat rising prices, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL growth exceed their specified thresholds, or average returns are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, increasing industrial production reflects a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, signaling improving corporate earnings and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery periods, INDPRO month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with SPX 60d returns (Rank IC < 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates robust job creation, boosting consumer confidence and spending expectations, which provides immediate positive sentiment to the equity market and drives short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS increases by over 200,000 in a month, or average returns are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Consistently high levels of housing starts reflect strong demand in the housing sector, which has significant multiplier effects across the economy (e.g., construction, retail, finance), leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) when HOUST is above its 5-year moving average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is very low and the Federal Funds Rate is elevated, it indicates an economy potentially overheating, prompting the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive monetary policy, which negatively impacts corporate valuations and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where UNRATE is below 4.0% and FEDFUNDS is above 3.0%, or average returns are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarterly GDP growth signifies a rapidly expanding economy, translating into higher corporate earnings and improved investor sentiment, which drives strong positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon as future growth prospects are discounted.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where annualized GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or average returns are not significantly positive.
